_meta.author: Claude (scorecard-refine-local autopilot, Adam-supervised)
_meta.note: LOCAL refinement batch — Hampton, VA officials: Hope Harper (At-Large Council), Jimmy Gray (Mayor), Michelle Ferebee (At-Large Council), Randy Bowman (At-Large Council), Steven Brown (Vice Mayor / Council). Hampton elections are officially nonpartisan; all five ran/serve as Independents on the ballot. All TRUE/FALSE cite a source URL; null where no evidence. Prior speculative party-default cells reset to null. High-bar discipline applied: vague slogans → null; institutional city policy (LGBTQ liaison, Phoebus Pride sponsorship) not attributed to individual members absent a personal vote/statement; 'publicly backed law enforcement' (public_justice q0) requires AFFIRMATIVE ADVOCACY, not merely voting a routine ordinance; economic_stewardship q2 left null on routine majority budget votes per the Wolfenbarger/FXBG precedent — only documented fiscal-restraint dissent earns TRUE.

profile.religion: Member, Bethel Temple Church (Hampton) — Assemblies of God Pentecostal congregation
profile.education: Kecoughtan High School (Hampton), 1991 (honor graduate); B.S. Information Systems & Decision Sciences, Virginia State University, 1996
profile.background: Hope Lorraine Harper; lifelong Hamptonian; Property Support Control Specialist at Northrop Grumman Corporation since 1998 (started as a system analyst). Co-author of 'The Lioness Leads: Stories of Triumph Over Fear.' Civic portfolio: Hampton Planning Commission; VML Finance Committee; Hampton Redevelopment & Housing Authority Council Liaison 2025-26; board of Hampton Roads Community Action Program; VP of Y.H. Thomas Community Center; We Are 100 board; past member Citizens Police Advisory Group; Delta Sigma Theta Sorority; Chums Inc. First won a Nov 2022 special election with 57.2%. Re-elected Nov 5, 2024 — led all 9 candidates with 18.62%. Current second term Jan 1, 2025 – Dec 31, 2028. Hampton City Council is officially nonpartisan; she ran as an Independent on the ballot. No spouse/children publicly disclosed. Distinguished from 'Sheila Hindman Harper' and 'Hope Hoffman.'

hope-harper.notes_append: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: At-Large Hampton City Council member, second term Jan 1, 2025 – Dec 31, 2028; re-elected Nov 5, 2024 leading 9 candidates with 18.62%. Hampton council is officially nonpartisan; she ran as Independent on the ballot. THIN ISSUE FOOTPRINT — campaign site (hope4hampton.org) has no policy/Issues page; WAVY-stated priorities are 'more opportunities for youth and families,' 'lowering gun violence,' and 'adding more employment' — generic. RELIGIOUS IDENTITY: member of Bethel Temple Church, an Assemblies of God Pentecostal congregation; church affiliation is on her official Hampton.gov bio. INSTITUTIONAL ROLES: serves on Hampton Planning Commission AND the Citizens Police Advisory Group AND Hampton Redevelopment & Housing Authority — an institutional-insider profile. DOCUMENTED VOTES: (a) FY26 Hampton budget May 2025 ($677.9M, 1¢ real-estate tax-rate cut from $1.15 → $1.14/$100, 6-1 with Campbell the lone NO) — Harper was in the 6-vote YES majority by elimination; routine governance vote that includes a tax cut already engineered by staff is NOT scored as fiscal-restraint advocacy per the rubric high-bar (consistent with the Wolfenbarger precedent). (b) April 2026 camping/sleeping-on-public-property ordinance passed 5-2 (Class 2 misdemeanor); Vice Mayor Brown and CM Ferebee were the named NOs — Harper was in the YES majority by elimination, but this is a public-order ordinance, not affirmative pro-LE advocacy per the rubric q0 bar. (c) Hampton University polling-site approval (unanimous council) — ballot-access expansion, not an integrity measure. ON GUN VIOLENCE (per WAVY 2024 candidate profile): she framed it as 'a national issue that has impacted [our] communities greatly' and called for 'a community effort' and 'proven methods' — vague community-effort framing, not affirmative pro-LE advocacy and not enough to flag the rubric's public-health framing under q3. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity_of_life, biblical_marriage (Hampton sponsors Phabulous Phoebus Pride and has an LGBTQ+ liaison, but Harper has no individual vote/statement on a proclamation or trans policy), family_child_sovereignty (no school-choice/parental-notification/CRT/SOGI statement; Hampton schools are a separate elected body), christian_liberty (Bethel Temple AG membership is identity, not a public-square Christian-symbols/prayer/conscience-rights statement), economic_stewardship (routine YES on a 6-1 budget with a rate cut already in the package is not fiscal-restraint advocacy in the rubric q2 sense — distinguish from Campbell's lone-NO dissent demanding more cuts), election_integrity, public_justice (institutional CPAG service and inferred YES on camping ordinance are not affirmative pro-LE advocacy per the high bar), refuse_state_overreach, border_immigration (Hampton rejected DHS sanctuary designation in May 2025 via Mayor Gray's letter, but Harper made no individual statement), self_defense.

profile.religion: Board member, Providence Bible College and Theological Seminary (Hampton); board member, Bernadine Franciscan Sisters Foundation (Catholic-affiliated). No personal church membership publicly disclosed.
profile.education: A.A.S. Tidewater Community College; B.S. Hampton University; M.P.A. Troy University
profile.background: James 'Jimmy' Gray Jr.; age ~69 at time of 2024 election. Retired public-safety executive: former Hampton Fire Chief (6 years) and former Hampton Assistant City Manager for Public Safety & Human Services (retired 2014); 26 prior years with Chesapeake Fire Department. Four children, three grandchildren. Affiliations: NAACP Hampton Branch, Sigma Pi Phi (the Boulé). Civic: chairs the Virginia Municipal League Legislative Committee; long Hampton-Newport News regional service. Prior Hampton City Council member 2016, 2018, 2022 terms; Vice Mayor 2018-2024. Elected Mayor of Hampton Nov 5, 2024 over retired Hampton fire captain Richelle Wallace ~70%-29%. Sworn in Jan 8, 2025 at Hampton Roads Convention Center. Current term Jan 1, 2025 – Dec 31, 2028. Hampton elections are officially nonpartisan; he filed and ran as Independent (VPAP). Not up again until 2028. Distinguished from any other 'Jimmy/James Gray.'

jimmy-gray.notes_append: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Mayor of Hampton; elected Nov 5, 2024 (~70% over Richelle Wallace); sworn in Jan 8, 2025; current term ends Dec 31, 2028; next election 2028. Hampton officially nonpartisan; filed as Independent (VPAP committee 'Gray for Hampton City Mayor – Jimmy'). HARD evidence: (1) On MAY 30, 2025 issued a FORMAL LETTER on Hampton city letterhead rejecting the U.S. Department of Homeland Security's placement of Hampton on the federal 'sanctuary jurisdictions' list. Hampton.gov posted the scanned PDF (CreationDate Fri May 30 16:23:13 2025 EDT, Ricoh IM C4510 scanner). Gray on the record (WTKR): 'Our city council has not adopted any ordinances, policies or resolutions, classifying ourselves as a sanctuary city... our police department and the city are cooperating when [we] are requested to assist with any execution of warrants or other activities involving Homeland Security agents working in our city.' → border_immigration q2 TRUE (affirmative rejection of sanctuary policy + endorsed federal-preemption posture). (2) Joint press briefing with Hampton Police Chief Jimmie Wideman on the surge in violence — Mayor Gray publicly partnered with the Chief, told residents to 'lock up your guns around your house, so if you have a break in, they can't steal your weapons,' and praised state-police partnership on 'street interdiction and traffic enforcement' as 'very effective' at getting 'guns and drugs off the street.' Combined with FY26 budget priorities to fund competitive police salaries — affirmative pro-LE advocacy bar met → public_justice q0 TRUE. (3) FY26 Hampton budget May 2025 ($677.9M, 1¢ real-estate tax-rate cut to $1.14/$100, 6-1 with Campbell the lone NO) — Gray was in the YES majority and publicly defended pay raises for city employees; this is routine governance with growing topline spending (5.4%), not fiscal-restraint advocacy in the rubric q2 sense → null on economic_stewardship q2 per the Wolfenbarger high-bar precedent. (4) April 2026 camping/sleeping-on-public-property ordinance (5-2 YES, with Brown/Ferebee NO) — Gray voted YES and framed it as continuing to be 'the compassionate community we strive to be'; he said the ordinance 'would be applied evenly to anyone in violation' — public-order ordinance, NOT affirmative pro-LE advocacy by itself. DEVIATION FLAGS: (a) Gray serves on the boards of Providence Bible College & Theological Seminary AND Bernadine Franciscan Sisters Foundation — religious-board service is real but does not by itself satisfy christian_liberty q0 (high-bar public-square Christian profession requires an active public profession like Anton Bell's Sixth Commandment court argument; Gray has not made one in available sources) → christian_liberty cells null. (b) Hampton city government earned a 100/100 HRC Municipal Equality Index score and sponsors the Phabulous Phoebus Pride Festival — Gray has NOT personally signed a Pride proclamation that is documented in available sources; the sponsorship is institutional/CVB-led, so biblical_marriage and christian_liberty q4 are null pending a direct personal-signature finding. (c) On Feb 27, 2026 Gray accepted the Virginia Center for Inclusive Communities (Peninsula) Humanitarian Award and framed his work as 'building bridges, creating space at the table, and ensuring that no child, and no adult, feels unseen or unheard' — diversity/inclusion language but specifically racial/ethnic/religious framing, not LGBTQ-specific → null. (d) State of the City Dec 3, 2025 'Level Up' speech praised Hampton City Schools' graduation rates; not a curriculum/parental-rights statement → null. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity_of_life, biblical_marriage q0/q2/q4 (no marriage/sexuality policy statement and no personally signed Pride proclamation located), family_child_sovereignty (no school-choice/parental-notification/CRT/SOGI statement), christian_liberty q0/q2/q3/q4 (board service to religious institutions is biographical, not active public-square advocacy), economic_stewardship q2 (routine budget YES with 5.4% growth not fiscal-restraint advocacy), election_integrity, public_justice q1/q2/q3/q4 (no bail-reform/restitution/public-health-framing/sword-of-state statement; Gray's gun-violence framing is shared-responsibility, not specifically magistrate-sword), refuse_state_overreach (chairs VML Legislative Committee but no specific Dillon-Rule pushback documented), border_immigration q1 (no statement on deportation of minors), self_defense (gun-violence framing emphasizes secure storage but no 2A-rights affirmation and no anti-2A position either).

0.note: Joint public press briefing with Hampton Police Chief Jimmie Wideman addressing the surge in violence — Mayor Gray publicly partnered with the Chief, praised state-police partnership on 'street interdiction and traffic enforcement' as 'very effective' at getting 'guns and drugs off the street'; previously served as Hampton Fire Chief and Assistant City Manager for Public Safety. FY26 budget defended competitive public-safety salaries ('we've got to provide a decent salary and benefits'). Affirmative pro-LE advocacy bar met — sustained public partnership + advocacy + concrete personnel investment, distinct from a routine budget YES.

2.note: On May 30, 2025 Mayor Gray issued a formal letter on Hampton city letterhead rejecting the U.S. Department of Homeland Security's placement of Hampton on the federal 'sanctuary jurisdictions' list, on the record: 'Our city council has not adopted any ordinances, policies or resolutions, classifying ourselves as a sanctuary city... our police department and the city are cooperating when [we] are requested to assist with any execution of warrants or other activities involving Homeland Security agents working in our city.' Hampton's regional jail continues to house ICE detainees. Affirmative opposition to sanctuary-city policy with explicit endorsement of federal preemption / cooperation.

profile.religion: Active member, St. Vincent de Paul Catholic Church (Hampton); member of the Knights of Peter Claver Ladies Auxiliary (Catholic lay organization)
profile.education: Bethel High School (Hampton); B.S. Mathematics, College of William & Mary; graduate coursework in Computer Science, William & Mary
profile.background: Michelle Taylor Ferebee; Hampton native. Retired Deputy Director, Office of Strategic Analysis, Communications & Business Development at NASA Langley Research Center (42+ years at NASA Langley; Deputy Director since May 2020). Married to Melvin Ferebee Jr. (39 years); 3 children, 8 grandchildren. Civic portfolio: Hampton EDA member 2013-2021 (served as Chair); Hampton History Museum Board; Bernardine Franciscan Sisters Foundation board; Hampton Roads PDC. Memberships: NAACP Silver Life Member, Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, The Links Inc., Jack and Jill of America Lifetime Member. Appointed interim Hampton Council member Jan 12, 2022 to fill the seat of Chris Snead; won 2022 special election for the 2-year remainder; won Nov 5, 2024 special election with 57.67% over Chelsea Greenwood (24.25%) and Jeannie Belgrave (18.07%) for the partial term remaining on Jimmy Gray's seat after he moved up to Mayor. Current term Jan 1, 2025 – Dec 31, 2026. Hampton officially nonpartisan; ballot label Independent; her campaign site indicates she will seek re-election in November 2026. Distinguished from other 'Michelle Ferebee' name matches.

michelle-ferebee.notes_append: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: At-Large Hampton City Council member, current term Jan 1, 2025 – Dec 31, 2026 (special-election remainder of Jimmy Gray's seat after Gray moved up to Mayor); won Nov 5, 2024 special election with 57.67%. Hampton council is officially nonpartisan; ballot label Independent. Will be on the ballot Nov 2026 for re-election per her campaign site. RELIGIOUS IDENTITY: explicitly discloses 'Active member of St. Vincent de Paul Catholic Church' on her campaign About page — unusually direct religious disclosure for a nonpartisan Hampton candidate. HARD evidence: (1) APRIL 8, 2026 Hampton camping/sleeping-on-public-property ordinance passed 5-2 — Ferebee was one of the two NAMED NO votes (joined by Vice Mayor Steven Brown); she explained her NO on the record: 'After months of work, it represents a solution. But from our perspective, it's not the right solution.' Hampton NAACP opposed the ordinance. The ordinance is a Class 2 misdemeanor (up to 6 months / $1,000 fine) — public_justice q0 does NOT score her FALSE on this alone because the q0 bar is opposition to defunding/dismantling police, not opposition to a specific public-order ordinance; her NO appears framed in compassion/homelessness terms, not anti-LE terms. → public_justice q0 null. (2) FY26 Hampton budget May 2025 — Ferebee was in the 6-vote YES majority (Campbell lone NO); also asked specifically about grant-funded programs in the March 12, 2025 budget retreat — routine governance, NOT fiscal-restraint advocacy → economic_stewardship q2 null per the Wolfenbarger high-bar precedent. CAMPAIGN POSTURE: Issues page lists ONLY Livability / Technology & Innovation / Coastal Resilience — deliberately apolitical good-government profile with NO culture-war positioning in either direction. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity_of_life, biblical_marriage (Hampton sponsors Phabulous Phoebus Pride but Ferebee has no individual vote/statement on a proclamation, drag, or trans policy), family_child_sovereignty (no school-choice/parental-notification/CRT/SOGI statement; Hampton schools are a separate elected body), christian_liberty (open Catholic identity on campaign site is religious identification but not a public-square Christian-symbols/conscience-rights statement per the high bar — distinguish from Anton Bell's Sixth Commandment invocation in open court which earned christian_liberty q0 TRUE), economic_stewardship q2 (routine YES on a 6-1 budget is not fiscal-restraint advocacy), election_integrity, public_justice q1/q2/q3/q4 (camping NO is not in itself the contrary of any q0–q4 cell), refuse_state_overreach, border_immigration (Hampton rejected DHS sanctuary designation in May 2025 via Mayor Gray's letter; Ferebee made no individual statement), self_defense.

profile.education: Bethel High School (Hampton), 1983; currently pursuing Business Management degree at Norfolk State University
profile.background: Randy C. Bowman Sr.; age 59; Hampton native. CEO, Bowman's Soul N the Wall (2017-present) and I Scream U Scream (2024-present); prior 15 years Walmart distribution management (2002-2017) and 12 years Verizon management (1990-2002). Two grown children, three grandchildren. Civic affiliations: NAACP, Sierra Club, 100 Black Men, Kiwanis. Lost the 2022 Hampton City Council special election (42.0%, to Hope Harper). Elected At-Large Nov 5, 2024 with 17.03% (2nd of 9 candidates for 3 seats); sworn in Jan 8, 2025; current term Jan 1, 2025 – Dec 31, 2028. Hampton officially nonpartisan; ballot label Independent; his restaurant has hosted Hampton Democratic Committee meetings (per reporting). Campaign slogan 'Better Together With Bowman.' Distinguished from Jamaal Bowman (NY former US Rep), Shane Bowman (WA), and other 'Randy/Randall Bowman' name matches.

randy-bowman.notes_append: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: At-Large Hampton City Council member, first term Jan 1, 2025 – Dec 31, 2028; elected Nov 5, 2024 with 17.03% (2nd of 9 candidates for 3 seats); sworn in Jan 8, 2025. Hampton council is officially nonpartisan; ballot label Independent; restaurant hosted Hampton Democratic Committee meetings — center-left independent profile. RELIGIOUS IDENTITY: member of Gethsemane Baptist Church since 2012 per official city bio (biographical disclosure only — no public Christian profession from the dais located). HARD evidence: (1) WAVY 2024 candidate profile, on his own record: 'As a victim of family gun violence, I support and believe, there needs to be tighter regulations on gun laws.' Additionally reported he posts 'no guns allowed' signage at his restaurant. → self_defense q1 FALSE (explicit affirmative-statement support for tighter gun regulation is the rubric's contrary position to opposition to red-flag/magazine limits/AWB/registries). (2) FY26 Hampton budget May 2025 ($677.9M, 1¢ rate cut to $1.14/$100, 6-1 with Campbell the lone NO) — Bowman in the YES majority by elimination; routine governance, not fiscal-restraint advocacy → economic_stewardship q2 null. (3) April 2026 camping ordinance 5-2 — Bowman in the YES majority by elimination (named NOs were Brown and Ferebee); public-order ordinance, not affirmative pro-LE advocacy → public_justice q0 null. (4) Southern Comfort permit-revocation unanimous council vote (2026) — Bowman quote: 'I'm a firm believer in second chances' (context: reluctant YES). DEVIATION FLAGS: (a) The pro-gun-regulation statement is the single strongest single-cell signal in his record; it is unambiguous, direct, and on his own record. (b) Top stated 2024 campaign priorities (per WAVY): economic growth, business incubator one-stop-shop, attracting chain restaurants/hotels for tourism revenue — pro-business but not specifically fiscal restraint. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity_of_life, biblical_marriage (Hampton sponsors Phabulous Phoebus Pride; no Bowman individual vote/statement), family_child_sovereignty (no school-choice/parental-notification/CRT/SOGI statement; Hampton schools are a separate elected body), christian_liberty (Gethsemane Baptist church membership is biographical, not a public-square Christian-symbols/profession statement per the high bar), economic_stewardship q2, election_integrity, public_justice q0/q1/q2/q3/q4 (no pro-LE affirmative statement; no bail/restitution/sword-of-state position), refuse_state_overreach, border_immigration (Hampton rejected DHS sanctuary designation in May 2025 via Mayor Gray's letter; Bowman made no individual statement), self_defense q0 (no specific constitutional-carry statement located).

1.note: WAVY 2024 candidate profile, on his own record: 'As a victim of family gun violence, I support and believe, there needs to be tighter regulations on gun laws.' Additionally reported he posts 'no guns allowed' signage at his restaurant. Affirmative support for tighter gun regulation is the rubric's contrary position to opposition to red-flag laws, magazine limits, AWB, and registries.

profile.religion: Ministry credentials and church affiliations: B.A. Biblical Studies, M.A. Ministry (Carolina University of Theology); honorary doctorate in Humanitarianism (The Great Commission Theological Seminary). Listed church associations: Calvary Community Church; Victory Life Church.
profile.education: Pembroke High School (Hampton); B.A. Biblical Studies; M.A. Ministry, Carolina University of Theology; Honorary Doctorate in Humanitarianism, The Great Commission Theological Seminary
profile.background: Steven L. Brown (also reported as Steven Lavonne Brown). Hampton native; Pembroke HS graduate. Professional background includes Riverside Health Systems, Calvary Community Church, Emerging Leadership Academy, and Victory Life Church. Wife Terry Brown; two adult children. First elected At-Large to Hampton City Council July 1, 2018; re-elected Nov 8, 2022; current second term Jan 1, 2023 – Dec 31, 2026. Selected Vice Mayor by council vote 4-3 on Jan 8, 2025 (succeeding Jimmy Gray when Gray became Mayor). Committee service: Hampton-Newport News Community Services Board (Chair); Hampton Roads PDC Commissioner; Citizen Engagement Advisory and Review Commission; Citizens Police Advisory Group; Virginia Housing Commission subcommittee. Hampton officially nonpartisan; ballot label Independent. Campaign Facebook tagline (indexed snippet): 'leadership for a more progressive Hampton.' Up for re-election in November 2026. Distinguished from Steve Brown of Key Life radio, accesso CEO Steve Brown, COO Steven Brown of Next Step Therapeutic, and other name matches.

steven-brown.notes_append: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Vice Mayor (selected by council 4-3 on Jan 8, 2025) and At-Large Hampton City Council member; second council term Jan 1, 2023 – Dec 31, 2026 (re-elected Nov 8, 2022; first elected July 1, 2018). Hampton officially nonpartisan; ballot label Independent. On the Nov 2026 ballot for re-election. RELIGIOUS PROFILE: B.A. Biblical Studies + M.A. Ministry + honorary doctorate + church associations (Calvary Community, Victory Life) — formal seminary/ministry credentials, but no public profession of Christ from the dais, no invocation/Sabbath/marriage stance, and no biblical-worldview policy statement located in available sources — notable absence for someone with formal credentials. HARD evidence: (1) APRIL 8, 2026 Hampton camping/sleeping-on-public-property ordinance passed 5-2 — Brown was one of the two NAMED NO votes (joined by CM Michelle Ferebee). No direct on-record Brown quote located explaining the NO; Ferebee's adjacent quote ('not the right solution') is the only contemporaneous rationale on the record — public-order ordinance, not framed as anti-police, so public_justice q0 stays null. (2) WAVY 2022 candidate profile, on his own record, framed youth gun violence as 'a national health crisis' and prescribed conflict-resolution training: 'I strongly believe we reduce gun violence when we teach our youth and adults to learn how to resolve conflict versus reacting by picking up a gun and settling your differences.' → public_justice q3 FALSE (explicit framing of violent crime — youth gun violence — as a 'national health crisis' is exactly the public-health framing the rubric q3 singles out as the contrary position). (3) FY26 Hampton budget May 2025 — Brown was in the 6-vote YES majority (Campbell lone NO); routine governance, not fiscal-restraint advocacy → economic_stewardship q2 null. CAMPAIGN POSTURE: Facebook page tagline (indexed snippet) reads 'leadership for a more progressive Hampton' — soft left self-identification, but the literal phrase alone does not satisfy any specific rubric cell. DEVIATION FLAGS: (a) The seminary/ministry credentials with NO documented public profession is a notable absence; the rubric's high bar (FXBG/Bell precedent) requires active public-square profession, which Brown has not delivered in available sources. (b) Public-health framing on gun violence is the single strongest single-cell signal in his record. (c) The April 2026 camping NO joined the local NAACP-aligned dissent against criminalization of homelessness — not a defunding-police statement. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity_of_life, biblical_marriage (Hampton sponsors Phabulous Phoebus Pride; no Brown individual vote/statement), family_child_sovereignty (no school-choice/parental-notification/CRT/SOGI statement; campaigned 2018 on fully funding K-12 — generic), christian_liberty (formal ministry credentials are biographical/educational, not active public-square Christian-symbols/profession statement per the high bar), economic_stewardship q2, election_integrity, public_justice q0/q1/q2/q4 (no pro-LE affirmative statement; no bail/restitution/sword-of-state position), refuse_state_overreach, border_immigration (Hampton rejected DHS sanctuary designation in May 2025 via Mayor Gray's letter; Brown made no individual statement), self_defense (gun-violence framing is public-health/conflict-resolution; no specific constitutional-carry or anti-red-flag/anti-AWB statement located).

3.note: WAVY 2022 candidate profile, on his own record: framed youth gun violence as 'a national health crisis' and prescribed conflict-resolution training as the prevention strategy ('I strongly believe we reduce gun violence when we teach our youth and adults to learn how to resolve conflict versus reacting by picking up a gun and settling your differences.'). Explicit 'health crisis' framing of violent crime is the rubric's defining contrary position to refusing to treat violent crime as a public-health excuse.
